Title: France's dilapidated National Museum of Natural History calls for help: 'The mineralized bones literally are exploding'
Subhead: The institution boasts strong visitor numbers and an international scientific reputation. But its head warns of catastrophic conditions, including mold in the collections, a flooded library, and cracks in the walls.

Kevin Warsh, Trump's Fed nominee, faces Senate scrutiny
Subhead: Nominated by the White House to succeed Jerome Powell as head of the United States central bank, Kevin Warsh has pledged to act with complete independence if confirmed by the Senate, a prospect that remains far from certain.

Title: Who is John Ternus, Tim Cook's successor as Apple CEO?
Subhead: A hands-on leader known for his calm management style, Apple's future CEO must now prove his ability to drive innovation.

Title: World Cup fans face soaring prices as transport costs skyrocket in the US
Subhead: Massachusetts and New Jersey transit authorities have announced dramatic fare hikes for round-trip train journeys to stadiums. The move has drawn sharp criticism from fans, already grappling with steep costs to attend the tournament.

Title: Why colonial-era Congo archives in Belgium are caught in a standoff over strategic minerals
Subhead: Accusations of 'digital colonialism' and capitalist predation are at the heart of a standoff between an American mining company and a Belgian public museum, as interest in the Democratic Republic of Congo's vast mineral wealth intensifies.

'Why am I alive?': The Yazidi genocide as told by one of its survivors
Subhead: Samih Taha, now a refugee in France, is one of the few survivors of the Islamic State group massacre of his village in northern Iraq on August 15, 2014. Then 17 years old, he went into debt to buy back his mother and sisters, who had been forced into sexual slavery. He told Le Monde his story and the tragedy that befell his family.

Title: France's print media in crisis amid massive layoffs
Subhead: Since December 2025, magazine and regional press companies have announced nearly 1,000 job cuts, putting the viability of countless newsrooms at risk.

Title: Nathalie Baye, acclaimed French actress with vast filmography
Subhead: Throughout her career, Baye intentionally took on a wide range of lead and supporting roles, portraying upper-class women to sex workers in both comedies and romantic dramas. She died on Friday at the age of 77 after a battle with Lewy body disease.
